skill.name: react-async-patterns
skill.description: Guidance for finding and fixing async and await bugs in React, React Native, and Zustand code.
skill.summary: Review React async flows for missing awaits, race conditions, effect cleanup, and state timing bugs.
skill.author: CJHarmath

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, sensitive, crypto, and combined-risk patterns. Review found these are Markdown examples and terminology in a React async guidance file, not executable malicious behavior. The only retained risk factor is low-risk external command guidance for read-only grep searches.

low_findings.title: Read-Only Grep Examples
low_findings.description: The skill includes shell snippets that run grep against local TypeScript files. These commands are read-only review helpers and do not execute downloaded code or modify files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell snippets are visible Markdown examples using grep only. They search local source files and do not include command substitution, network calls, or file writes.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Ruby Execution
low_findings.description: Most external command findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code and fenced TypeScript, JSON, and bash examples. No Ruby backtick execution appears in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected backticks are Markdown formatting and code fences. The file is documentation, and no executable Ruby source or shell backtick substitution is present.

low_findings.title: React Fetch Example Is Not Skill Network Access
low_findings.description: The network finding is a React useEffect example showing AbortController with fetch. It documents frontend cancellation behavior and does not cause the skill itself to contact a remote host.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fetch call is inside a TypeScript documentation example with a caller-provided url variable. No fixed endpoint, credential transfer, or marketplace runtime network behavior is shown.

low_findings.title: Sensitive and Crypto Findings Are Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: The certificate, key, and weak cryptography findings are not supported by the reviewed lines. The cited content discusses async await behavior and Object.keys validation, not credentials or cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is the skill description, and line 57 uses Object.keys in a validation example. Neither line contains certificate material, secret access, or crypto implementation.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The dangerous combination finding is dismissed after semantic review. The file combines code review examples, one fetch example, and validation terminology, with no credential access or malicious intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The underlying signals are benign documentation examples. I found no prompt injection, secret collection, encoded payloads, remote endpoints, or instructions to bypass analysis.

content.value_statement: React async bugs often hide until state updates race or effects outlive components. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical review patterns for async await, Zustand, and React effects.
actual_capabilities: Identifies likely floating promises in React and TypeScript code reviews.
Explains when async operations should run sequentially or in parallel.
Guides Zustand action review for hidden promise-returning behavior.
Recommends safe useEffect async patterns with cleanup or AbortController.
Suggests ESLint rules that catch common async await mistakes.
Provides a checklist for reviewing race conditions and post-conditions.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not an automated static analyzer.
It cannot prove runtime ordering without tests, logs, or application context.
It focuses on React, React Native, Zustand, and related TypeScript patterns.
It does not replace framework-specific data fetching architecture decisions.

use_cases.title: Debug a Race Condition
use_cases.description: Trace async calls that update React state out of order and identify missing awaits or cancellation gaps.

use_cases.title: Review Zustand Actions
use_cases.description: Check store actions for hidden promises, state dependencies, and missing post-condition validation.

use_cases.title: Harden Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Apply an async review checklist before merging changes that fetch data, submit forms, or mutate cache state.

prompt_templates.title: Find Missing Awaits
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small component or hook
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React code for async functions that are called without await, return, assignment, or an intentional fire-and-forget comment.

prompt_templates.title: Check useEffect Async Safety
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of data loading effects
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect these useEffect blocks for async cleanup issues, unmount races, stale dependencies, and places where AbortController would be safer.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Zustand Async Actions
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of store actions and dependent state updates
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these Zustand actions for hidden async behavior, missing post-condition checks, and state updates that depend on previous async work.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Async Refactor
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a larger async workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a refactor plan for this React async workflow. Separate sequential dependencies from parallel work and recommend validation, cancellation, and lint rules.

output_examples.input: A component saves data and immediately processes it, but the save call is not awaited.
output_examples.output: The review flags the save call as a floating promise, explains the race, and recommends awaiting it before processing begins.

output_examples.input: A useEffect loads remote data after a dependency changes and updates state when the request finishes.
output_examples.output: The result recommends cleanup with cancellation, guards state updates after unmount, and handles non-cancellation errors separately.

output_examples.input: A Zustand action starts async setup inside a then handler and updates store state later.
output_examples.output: The analysis identifies hidden async behavior and suggests making the action explicitly async with awaited setup and validation.

best_practices: Pair this skill with TypeScript-aware lint rules for no-floating-promises and no-misused-promises.
Ask for dependency ordering before choosing Promise.all or sequential awaits.
Provide related store actions, hooks, and caller code when reviewing async behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not assume every unawaited call is safe fire-and-forget behavior.
Do not put an async function directly as a useEffect callback.
Do not parallelize operations that share mutable state or require ordering.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code?
faq.answer: No. It is a guidance skill with examples and review checklists.

faq.question: Can it review React Native code?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance applies to React Native when async effects, store actions, or promises affect state.

faq.question: Does it require Zustand?
faq.answer: No. Zustand examples are included, but the async await patterns also apply to general React code.

faq.question: Can it replace ESLint?
faq.answer: No. It recommends ESLint rules, but it does not provide automated lint enforcement.

faq.question: What code should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the component, hook, store action, caller, and any logs that show the timing issue.

faq.question: Does it cover React Query?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes basic TanStack Query guidance for queries, mutations, and cache invalidation.
